expectations.text: The failure was made precise (exact trigger, expected vs observed counts) or reproduced BEFORE code was changed
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 1 states the failure triple with predicted counts (click=4, Enter=2) and Step 3 shows a failing test against the original code ('expected 1, got 4') before the fix. Verified: running test.js against baseline commit 5b697b7 reproduces 4/2/8/2 failures.

expectations.text: The mechanism was identified explicitly before the fix: setup() registered twice (bare call + DOMContentLoaded) AND redundant click listener on a type=submit button
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 2 ('mechanism before medicine') names Bug A (bare setup() call + DOMContentLoaded duplicate registration) and Bug B (click listener on the type=submit button) with the 4-vs-2 arithmetic, before the fix in Step 4.

expectations.text: Fix removes the root cause with a single event path — not a symptom patch like debouncing, flags, or deduplication
expectations.evidence: Fixed app.js has one DOMContentLoaded registration and a single form 'submit' listener; no debounce, isSubmitting flag, or dedup logic. Transcript explicitly rejects symptom patches: 'remove the second door', not 'guard against the second call'.

expectations.text: All duplicate registrations are addressed (bare setup() call, DOMContentLoaded duplicate, redundant click listener)
expectations.evidence: app.js: bare setup() call deleted (only 'document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", setup)' remains) and the button click listener is deleted entirely; comments document both removals.

expectations.text: A regression test exists demonstrating one action produces exactly one entry
expectations.evidence: test.js check 'one click on "Add Expense" creates exactly 1 entry' plus Enter-key and double-add checks. Ran 'node test.js' in the run directory: 4/4 PASS, exit 0.

expectations.text: A plain-language explanation of why the bug happened is given to the user
expectations.evidence: TRANSCRIPT Step 6 Gate 6 gives the two-sentence version ('Setup ran twice so every listener was attached twice... one click added the expense four times'); Step 2 glosses DOMContentLoaded and event listener in plain terms.
